key_insight: LuckyStar 111B shows that a post-trained multilingual model can be efficiently adapted into a Korean-English tool-using agent by combining preamble-controlled reasoning with targeted fine-tuning, verifiable RL, and quantized deployment.
engineering_relevance: The paper offers a practical recipe for building enterprise agents that reason and call tools accurately while staying within memory and serving constraints on a single GPU.
method: Start from Cohere Command A instead of training from scratch
Use preamble conditioning to toggle between concise answers and longer tool-oriented reasoning
Apply multilingual SFT, RL with verifiable rewards, language-consistency rewards, and 4-bit quantization
evidence_signal: The abstract reports improved mathematical reasoning, function calling, and NL2SQL performance while preserving Korean and English instruction-following quality.
watch_item: The approach depends on careful reward design and quantized serving, so misaligned language-consistency objectives or quantization-induced accuracy loss could affect real deployments.
paper_title: Think in English, Answer in Korean: Efficient Adaptation of Multilingual Tool-Using Agents
